vulnerabilities.cve-description: The `cFS` module `mm` v2.5.99 contains the vulnerable code weakness CWE-123 which allows the user to write arbitrary data to areas of memory outside the scope of a policy. In this version of the module, the command `MM_DumpMemToFileCmd`, allows the user to input various parameters including source address and address range. These values are not validated to ensure they are in a valid memory region. These values are later used in a memcpy, allowing the user to copy protected memory outside the scope of the cFS process.

The `cFS` module `mm` v2.5.99 contains the vulnerable code weakness CWE-123 which allows the user to write arbitrary data to areas of memory outside the scope of a policy. In this version of the module, the command `MM_DumpInEventCmd`, allows the user to input various parameters including source address and address range. These values are not validated to ensure they are in a valid memory region. These values are later used in a memcpy, allowing the user to copy protected memory outside the scope of the cFS process.

The `cFS` module `mm` v2.5.99 contains the vulnerable code weakness CWE-123 which allows the user to write arbitrary data to areas of memory outside the scope of a policy. In this version of the module, the `MM_FillMemCmd` command is designed to allow the user to fill a memory block directly with a user-defined pattern. This can cause a denial of service by crashing the program due to invalid memory access.

The `cFS` module `mm` v2.5.99 contains the vulnerable code weakness CWE-123 which allows the user to write arbitrary data to areas of memory outside the scope of a policy. In this version of the module, the `MM_LoadMemWIDCmd` command contains a similar vulnerability to the previously described CVE for `MM_FillMemCmd`. The user can input an invalid memory range, causing the program to access invalid memory when loading data, causing a segmentation fault.
